item.title: Burrowing bettongs returned to outback decades after local extinction
item.description: Wiped off mainland Australia by the introduction of cats and foxes, burrowing bettongs are making a triumphant comeback in outback NSW.

item.title: Mount Gambier resident Ben Whitehead in remission after surprise cancer trial results
item.description: Ben Whitehead was given just months to live after being diagnosed with a rare cancer that doctors had not seen before. A second clinical trial has delivered hope.

item.title: US-Iran agreement confirms Trump trend on the art of the deal
item.description: The ceasefire agreement fits a pattern from Donald Trump where the signing of a deal appears to be the major goal and the details often seem a distant secondary consideration.

item.title: Gavin Newsom tells Trump to leave his wife out of investigations
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Allied health workers walk off the job in Victoria as pay negotiations break down
item.description: The strike involves radiographers, sonographers, physiotherapists, podiatrists, speech pathologists and social workers at the state's public hospitals.

item.title: US-Iran ceasefire deal shaped by China and Russia, experts say
item.description: Chinese, Russian and Iranian officials met about a day before the deal was publicly announced, with experts saying it proves Beijing and Moscow helped shape the agreement.

item.title: Pensioner veers into riders causing terrifying crash at Germany's LVM Saarland Trofeo
item.description: An elderly woman on a mobility scooter has caused a frightening crash during a cycling race in Germany after riding into the path of the competitors.

item.title: Tasmanian police officer Keith Smith remembered at Ulverstone memorial service one year after he was killed
item.description: A Tasmanian police officer who was killed while on duty is remembered for his compassion and care during a memorial service, one year after his death.

item.title: Emotional tributes given at memorial for Tasmanian Constable Keith Smith
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: South Australia's Electoral Commissioner Mick Sherry resigns
item.description: South Australia's Electoral Commissioner Mick Sherry has resigned amid a review into the scandal-plagued state and Voice elections.

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz is expected to finally reopen within days. Then what?
item.description: The months-long shutdown of the the crucial shipping route has rocked global oil markets and flowed through to all sorts of prices. How will the reopening change things, and how fast?

item.title: Solomon Islands prime minister raises Pacific security pact proposal during Australia visit
item.description: Solomon Islands's new prime minister raises the idea of a Pacific-wide security treaty while meeting Prime Minister Anthony Albanese this month.

item.title: Artemis III pilot Luca Parmitano recalls witnessing 'devastating' Black Summer bushfires from space
item.description: Luca Parmitano was commanding the International Space Station when bushfires devastated eastern Australia. He has a blunt warning for the future of the planet.

item.title: How having older parents transformed my life at a young age
item.description: Having older parents has shaped my life in many ways, including taking on a carer role for Mum and Dad in my 20s.

item.title: Police receive 'new information' about Brisbane doctor charged with almost 100 counts of rape
item.description: Former Brisbane GP Stellios Theodoros is facing 148 counts of rape and sexual assault relating to an alleged excessive number of pelvic examinations, pap smears and breast checks.

item.title: Police investigate footage of officer allegedly pushing female Pro-Palestine protester
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NSW Police investigate video of officer pushing woman at Wollongong Bisalloy protest
item.description: NSW Police will review footage showing a woman falling to the ground after being pushed by an officer during a pro-Palestine protest outside Bisalloy Steels in Wollongong.

item.title: On the last big day, Leichhardt Oval showed its power one more time
item.description: On the day of the final NRL game at Leichhardt Oval before the old ground changes forever, rugby league's broken-down palace reminded us all of its greatest powers.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Cabo Verde finds player on LinkedIn, meet Merlin the Duck
item.description: Cabo Verde's incredible result against Spain brings with it no shortage of amazing stories, while a duck in a jersey is stealing hearts in Mexico. Here are today's quick hits from the World Cup.

item.title: KPMG referred to anti-corruption watchdog amid three-month freeze on Commonwealth contracts
item.description: KPMG is facing a three-month freeze on new federal government contracts amid a worsening scandal over the treatment of a whistleblower, with the Greens now referring the beleaguered company to the national anti-corruption watchdog.

item.title: Paedophile teacher sentenced for historical sex offences at Traralgon school
item.description: The court hears William Gilfillan, more commonly known as Rob, will be 92 years old when he becomes eligible for parole, and he will likely die in jail.

item.title: Emergency care delayed before death of wellness influencer Stacey Warnecke, court hears
item.description: A court probing the death of Stacey Warnecke after a freebirth has heard that emergency care was delayed for several minutes because her "birth keeper" would only call an ambulance if she consented.

item.title: Retailer Lincraft to close remainder of shopfronts and move entirely online
item.description: Retailer Lincraft will close the remainder of its shopfronts after more than 80 years of providing fabric, materials and homewares to Aussie crafters.

item.title: World Cup: After complicated build-up, Iran finally plays amid mixed feelings from diaspora
item.description: After a challenging, politically charged build-up to their World Cup campaign Iran's 2-2 draw with New Zealand brought mixed feelings for the Iranian diaspora.

item.title: Police officer says rescuing dog from burning South Burnett home all in a day's work
item.description: A fast-acting police officer has saved a feisty Chihuahua from a burning home in the South Burnett.

item.title: Detectives investigating Dezi Freeman's movements after police killings arrest two men
item.description: Police executed seven warrants at properties across Victoria and New South Wales this morning.

item.title: Trump's 'American flag blue' Reflecting Pool turns green after $20m project completion
item.description: A $20 million renovation of Washington, DC's Reflecting Pool set out to fix leaks and turn the water blue. Mother Nature had other plans.

item.title: Visitors react to algae in Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: El Niño declared by BOM and it could become the strongest on record
item.description: El Niño is a state that can linger for up to 12 months and disrupt weather patterns across the globe, with Australia especially prone.

item.title: Man arrested after allegedly assaulting four cops, biting two, during arrest
item.description: A man has been arrested after he allegedly assaulted a woman and a group of police officers in regional South Australia.

item.title: Cameron Munster backs Maroons teammate Tino Fa'asuamaleaui to honour late dad in State of Origin II
item.description: Queensland captain Cameron Munster is confident prop Tino Fa'asuamaleaui will do his late father Fereti proud in tomorrow night's State of Origin II at the MCG.

item.title: Townsville Mayor Nick Dametto breaks silence on island assault investigation
item.description: Queensland police are investigating an alleged altercation between the 42-year-old councillor and a 28-year-old man.

item.title: 'Global' midwife shortage temporarily shuts Crystal Brook and Jamestown birthing services in SA's Mid North
item.description: Two more South Australian regional centres are now temporarily diverting expectant mothers as Kapunda Hospital's birthing service is closed indefinitely.

item.title: Meet the Iranian women who support the regime and see Trump as the villain
item.description: As we journey into the heart of Iran, we meet the women who support the clerics and want revenge on the man who killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Terella Brewing fined following legal battle with Sunshine Coast Council
item.description: A once popular brewery has been fined in court after shutting up shop earlier this year due to an ongoing legal battle with a Queensland council.

item.title: Blues coach Laurie Daley reveals Ethan Strange's role in State of Origin II against Maroons at MCG
item.description: After his starring role for the Blues in State of Origin I, Ethan Srange is given a new assignment against the Maroons at the MCG on Wednesday night.

item.title: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor meets Kumanjayi Little Baby's grandmother in Alice Springs
item.description: Federal Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has visited Alice Springs, where he has met family of Kumanjayi Little Baby and renewed the Coalition's calls for deeper scrutiny of federal funding directed to the region.

item.title: Alice Springs branch of Victims of Crime NT to close down down amid funding insecurity
item.description: The Alice Springs branch of Victims of Crime NT will be closed by the end of the week, with the organisation's chief executive citing funding insecurity. The closure will mean victims in the region will only be able to access services remotely.

item.title: Officer tells inquest he gave Far North Qld man one second to drop knife before fatal shot
item.description: A Special Emergency Response Team officer tells a coronial inquest it took one second or less to storm a bathroom and fire the shot that killed Far North Queensland man Aubrey Donahue during a hostage siege.

item.title: Iran deal says 'loud and clear' that Tehran will not have a nuclear weapon: Trump
item.description: Speaking on the sidelines of the G7 in France, Donald Trump says Iran will never have nuclear weapons.

item.title: Russian artist who satirised Vladimir Putin shot dead in Poland
item.description: Two Belarusian nationals are arrested in connection with the killing, authorities say.

item.title: G7 leaders agree to pressure Russia to end war with Ukraine
item.description: Donald Trump says Moscow should "make a deal" to end the war with Ukraine as G7 leaders have agreed to intensify pressure on Russia.

item.title: Russian frigate fires warning shots near UK waters to avert yacht collision
item.description: Britain and Russia have issued statements to confirm that a Russian frigate fired warning shots to divert a UK-flagged civilian yacht near British territorial waters on Tuesday.

item.title: ABC More or Less quiz: State of Origin Game II
item.description: Today's ABC More or Less quiz challenges you to go deep into AFL territory and come away with the win. Mouthguards in, let's go.

item.title: Pokémon Pokopia, Marathon, Mixtape and more — the best video games from the first half of 2026
item.description: From cosy builders to hectic shooters and all the way through to nostalgic adventures, there are lots of gems to enjoy on your favourite game console so far this year.

item.title: Ann Patchett rewrites the step-family narrative in her new novel, Whistler
item.description: The bestselling author of books including Bel Canto, Tom Lake and her latest, Whistler, is determined to see the good in everyone.

item.title: Shadow Treasurer Tim Wilson holds undisclosed shares in climate advisory firm through family trust
item.description: The shadow treasurer's investment trust holds 15,000 shares in a small company called Gondwana Carbon which helps developers to acquire land, develop carbon abatement projects, earn carbon credits and then trade them.

item.title: Fuel shock expected to be felt for years as businesses urge extended relief
item.description: Small-town coffee shops and long-established businesses are at risk of closing despite a ceasefire in the Middle East easing the global oil price. Regional businesses say fuel relief measures need to be extended because the damage done from the fuel shock is far from over.

item.title: Marauding cockatoos thwarted by bin lid invented by men's shed
item.description: Residents of the scenic Great Ocean Road are at their wits' end after the increasingly intelligent birds learnt how to lift the lids of wheelie bins, spreading rubbish through the town of Lorne.

item.title: What time is State of Origin II kick-off? How to watch Blues vs Maroons at MCG
item.description: NSW and Queensland meet at the famous MCG in State of Origin Game II. Here is what you need to know ahead of tonight's match.

item.title: What is in Donald Trump's Iran deal and what does Tehran hope to gain?
item.description: Iran agreeing to a ceasefire deal with the United States tells us Tehran likely had something to gain from signing on. This is what Iran is promoting as the 14-point deal.

item.title: Visitors to Hobart's Kunanyi/Mount Wellington should pay, tourism consultancy recommends
item.description: A tourism consultancy firm has recommended that visitors to Hobart's Kunanyi/Mt Wellington be charged for access — likely to result in a "deliberate and positive outcome" of fewer people going.

item.title: Gout Gout clocks personal best in 150m stoush with Noah Lyles at Ostrava Golden Spike
item.description: Rising Australian superstar Gout Gout pushes American champion Noah Lyles all the way in their first race against each other.

item.title: Australian pensioners finding rent and cost-of-living relief in moving overseas
item.description: Peter Stevenson says moving to Thailand has allowed him to live more comfortably on his Australian Age Pension after struggling to cover rent and essentials in regional Victoria.

item.title: Queensland government signs new long-term lease for 'life changing' school for autistic children
item.description: The private Sycamore School in Alexandra Hills is so sought after, some parents drive almost 50 kilometres one-way to drop off their kids, but it was "on the chopping block".

item.title: NDIS participants highlight mental toll of navigating 'complex' system
item.description: Participants say they are "exhausted" and "overwhelmed" by trying to navigate the complex and ever-changing NDIS system.

item.title: Interest-free loans and cash subsidies available to NSW residents for renewable energy uptake
item.description: Interest-free loans and cash subsidies are being offered by the New South Wales government to encourage the uptake of renewable energy measures like solar panels and household batteries.

item.title: How the explosion of the ultra-wealthy risks democracy
item.description: Democracy cannot survive trillionaires. We need to start taxing billionaires properly.

item.title: Childcare workers scrap walk-off as government commits to pay rise extension
item.description: Early educators had threatened to walk off the job to keep a federally funded 15 per cent pay subsidy due to end in November.

item.title: Mystery of saltwater crocodile dumped on side of Adelaide road continues
item.description: Reptile remains found dumped on the side of an Adelaide roadside have been confirmed as belonging to a saltwater crocodile, but there is still no explanation for how they came to be there.

item.title: Muswellbrook councillor manages shop that sells illegal tobacco
item.description: A councillor from the Upper Hunter in NSW manages a tobacco shop that sells illegal cigarettes as experts warn the black-market crisis is deepening in regional areas.

item.title: State of Origin coaches back referee Ashley Klein after gambling history made public
item.description: Billy Slater and Laurie Daley come to the defence of referee Ashley Klein after his gambling debt history was revealed earlier in the week.

item.title: Arsonists target Rare Breed gym in South Melbourne for the second day in a row
item.description: Police are investigating the second suspicious fire at a South Melbourne gym in as many days.

item.title: Jeremy Rockliff wants to move forward after cabinet resignations — but will the parliament let him?
item.description: Tasmania's powerful crossbench forced Madeleine Ogilvie and Jane Howlett out the door. Jeremy Rockliff says it's now time to "move forward".

item.title: Hanson lays out vision for 'monoculture' Australia, as GetUp! claims credit for banner stunt — as it happened
item.description: GetUp! says it is was responsible for the banner that unfurled during Pauline Hanson's first-ever address to the National Press Club.

item.title: Trump intensifies pressure on Netanyahu over Lebanon attacks
item.description: The US president levels more criticism at Israel's prime minister and suggests asking Syria to take on Hezbollah "if Israel can't do the job without killing everyone else".

item.title: Women forced to labour in RDH tearoom, run-down wards as NT government shuts down calls for new hospital
item.description: From a mother forced to labour in a hospital's tearoom, to a baby sent home with jaundice on a busy night, doctors warn they are no longer able to provide NT patients with the standard of care they deserve.

item.title: US-Iran agreement sparks fertiliser hope for farmers
item.description: Farmers are hoping the US-Iran deal will help free up fertiliser supply and lower costs during the winter cropping season.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands reaches settlement with ARN Media over termination of $100m contract
item.description: Veteran Sydney broadcaster Kyle Sandilands and ARN Media reach a settlement to end a $100 million contract dispute, which had been playing out in the Federal Court.

item.title: Lucky escape for Perth man allegedly caught with poisonous death cap mushrooms
item.description: A man dodges potentially fatal poisoning after allegedly mistaking lethal death cap mushrooms for their hallucinogenic counterparts while illegally foraging in southern Western Australia.

item.title: Kylian Mbappé denied penalty after 'nonsense' decision from A-League referee Alireza Faghani
item.description: Kylian Mbappé scores two more World Cup goals to bring his overall tally to 14, just two shy of the all time record. But the biggest talking point comes when A-League referee Alireza Faghani denies the French superstar a penalty, with the call slammed by experts.

item.title: More support urged for men's suicide prevention as advocates call for greater focus on situational distress
item.description: There are urgent calls for more customised support services for men and boys experiencing situational distress.

item.title: Queensland government accused of removing Aboriginal people from health roles in 'Project Invisibility'
item.description: The Queensland government will not deny the existence of "Project Invisibility", a name a barrister says is used internally to refer to the deliberate removal of Aboriginal people from leadership roles.

item.title: Police arrest teenager over alleged kidnapping murder of Sydney grandfather
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Two men charged over alleged murder of Chris Baghsarian, six now before the courts
item.description: Robbery and Serious Crime Squad detectives have charged another two men following an investigation into the alleged kidnapping and murder of Chris Baghsarian earlier this year in Sydney's north.

item.title: Bluey translated into an Indigenous language for the first time
item.description: The animated award-winning cartoon series Bluey has been translated into Yolŋu Matha, from north-east Arnhem Land, for NAIDOC Week.

item.title: ASIC launches criminal investigation into Zone RV as new allegations emerge
item.description: The former director of Zone RV faces a criminal probe into allegations he acted recklessly or dishonestly before the manufacturer collapsed with debts of $42 million.

item.title: Star casino's former CEO and lawyer penalised after Chinese money laundering scandal
item.description: Two former executives of The Star Entertainment Group Ltd have been handed penalties for breaching the Corporations Act in connection with a Chinese money-laundering scandal.

item.title: Organised crime gangs who recruit children could face life in jail under new Victorian laws
item.description: The Victorian government is raising the maximum penalty for recruiting children to commit crimes as it seeks to shut down organised crime syndicates driving the state's crime wave.

item.title: How police linked Sydney childcare worker David James to dark web posts
item.description: Investigators were knocking on the door of a disgraced Sydney childcare worker within a week of him posting images of children on the darknet after identifying the user through metadata and CCTV.

item.title: Runaway Gold Coast Tasmanian devil Mary recaptured after two-week search
item.description: A spokesperson for Village Roadshow Theme Parks said Mary, the two-year-old marsupial, was found and recaptured overnight.

item.title: Former Don Dale Youth Detention Centre inmates have payout for tear gas incident slashed by High Court
item.description: The High Court slashes an award for exemplary damages for four former inmates from Darwin's notorious Don Dale Youth Detention Centre who were affected by a tear gas incident in 2014.

item.title: Gina Rinehart's Hancock Iron Ore flags job losses at Pilbara operations
item.description: Gina Rinehart's Hancock Iron Ore has cut jobs a year after merging its two iron ore businesses into a single entity, but won't say how many.

item.title: G7 leaders could stand up to Donald Trump and swap flattery for frankness
item.description: In France, the US president finds himself among a group of emboldened counterparts, many of whom now seem more comfortable criticising him publicly than ever before.

item.title: Federal government announces more than half a billion dollars in sports funding ahead of Brisbane 2032 Games
item.description: The $513 million two-year package will include cost of living assistance for athletes, funding for dozens of sporting programs, and boosts for Winter and Paralympic athletes.

item.title: New SA laws would remove serious offenders' rights over victims' remains
item.description: The South Australian government will introduce legislation to remove the rights of perpetrators of serious crimes to decide what happens with the remains of their victims.

item.title: Two-thirds of scam compounds 'bypassed' in Cambodia's crackdown, Amnesty finds
item.description: A report by Amnesty International casts doubt on the impact of Cambodia's high-profile scam industry crackdown, finding authorities failed to dismantle the "vast majority" of operations.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Kylian Mbappé's banger, Marcelo Bielsa's morose pose and a full kit baby Jesus
item.description: Kylian Mbappé's magical strike thrust him back into the World Cup spotlight, while master coach Marcelo Bielsa did everything he could to avoid it. French journalists leave an empty space and Jesus gets a Mexican kit makeover. Here's the five quick hits from the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Rockhampton Zoo raises newborn chimp on human colostrum after mum accidentally drops infant
item.description: Rockhampton Zoo is hand-rearing a chimp whose mother dropped it from a height by accident hours after giving birth.

item.title: Dams are full at Quondong Station in South Australia.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Pastoralists take to the skies to track down livestock lost in floods
item.description: Closed roads and flooded plains are making livestock inaccessible for some pastoralists, while others are employing some serious technology to track down their animals.

item.title: Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé, Erling Haaland delight as World Cup stars come out to play
item.description: Lionel Messi scored a hat-trick, while Kylian Mbappé and Erling Haaland both scored braces on a night where the World Cup's biggest stars shone brightest.

item.title: Chimp baby
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Socceroos star Alessandro Circati reacts to USA warning
item.description: Socceroos defender Alessandro Circati offers a blunt reply to Sebastian Berhalter's comments ahead of the World Cup clash between the two sides.

item.title: Australia names Commonwealth Games netball squad for gold medal defence in Glasgow
item.description: The Diamonds include five players set to make their Commonwealth Games debut as they look to win their fifth gold medal in Glasgow.

item.title: Killer of Bridgette 'Biddy' Porter wanted to play serial killer game the day before killing, inquest hears
item.description: The mother of Biddy Porter's killer has told a coronial inquest she regrets not being able to get mental health support for her child before the killing.

item.title: Climate change adding uncertainty to El Niño impact, scientists say
item.description: El Niño is declared and scientists say the climate change is making its impacts harder to predict.

item.title: Kingston Fire Brigade delivered emergency vehicle with multiple defects
item.description: It's a $600,000 fire-fighting truck, the first of 15 to enter service in Tasmania, but a list of faults has sent it back for warranty repairs.

item.title: Andrew Thaler faces court over allegedly driving into neighbour's car
item.description: Andrew Thaler is facing five charges related to a verbal argument with a neighbour in Nimmitabel.

item.title: Kay Reid, AFL stars' mother, says home raided by Dezi Freeman investigators
item.description: Kay Reid, a regional Victorian businesswoman and the mother of footballers Ben and Sam Reid, confirms police searched her home as part of Taskforce Summit's investigation into the movements of police killer Dezi Freeman.

item.title: Six years after daughter's death, Kate Savage's mother has 'lost hope' in mental health reforms
item.description: The mother of Kate Savage, whose death at 13 sparked a review of mental health supports for children, says she's lost faith promises of reform will ever be kept.

item.title: Ngambri people say ACT government has not upheld agreement around recognition of traditional ownership
item.description: The ACT government has been accused of failing to uphold its agreement with the Ngambri people over the recognition of traditional ownership of the Canberra area.

item.title: England enjoying 'right to dream' about winning World Cup but refusing to be 'delusional'
item.description: As a World Cup favourite, England is pushing aside decades of heartbreak and disappointment to embrace the pressure of finally trying to bring football home again.

item.title: New Zealand's Tyler and Jenny Bindon become first World Cup mother-son duo
item.description: When Tyler Bindon came off the bench for New Zealand in its men's World Cup opener against Iran, he made a bit of history with his mother.

item.title: Father of Gold Coast teenager killed in e-bike crash speaks after driver sentenced
item.description: A Gold Coast father whose son died in an electric bike crash has spoken out after the driver was sentenced, saying the devices need better regulation.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Iraq's Aymen Hussein scores goal against Norway after being detained by US customs
item.description: Iraq striker Aymen Hussein was delayed on the way to his first World Cup, but he still made the best of his tournament debut.

item.title: Jeremy Clarkson says treatment has gone 'awry' after 'aggressive' prostate cancer diagnosis
item.description: The former Top Gear host shared the news on an episode of his Clarkson's Farm reality series, shortly before undergoing surgery that removed "10 per cent" of his prostate.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands's $12m settlement an 'exceptionally good deal' for ARN Media, economist says
item.description: In an ASX announcement on Wednesday, ARN Media agreed to pay the shock jock $12.09 million, with $3 million payable as soon as next month.

item.title: 13-year-old boy charged with violent extremist offences not released on bail
item.description: Police allege a Queensland teenager had written a manifesto expressing hatred towards children.

item.title: Alleged parent killer Raelene Polymiadis captured 'confessing' to murders, jury told
item.description: Accused murderer Raelene Polymiadis was allegedly captured on police listening devices confessing to killing her elderly parents while praying, a court has heard.

item.title: Two children die after being hit by car in Cabramatta in Sydney's south-west
item.description: Two children have died after being struck by a car while sitting in a pram in Sydney's south-west.

item.title: Pauline Hanson wasn't there for the National Press Club. She was there for Fight Club
item.description: The spectacle of the One Nation leader having a blue with journalists — or indeed anyone even vaguely associated with Canberra incumbency — is catnip for the Hanson algorithm. And the lady is not stupid.

item.title: Pauline Hanson lays out vision to end multiculturalism in press club address crashed by GetUp
item.description: A banner interrupted the senator’s first address at the National Press Club in 30 years, in which she laid out a vision for an Australian "monoculture".

item.title: NT government climate resilience plan prioritises acceleration of Beetaloo gas project
item.description: The Northern Territory government's newly released climate resilience plan has listed the acceleration of gas extraction at the Beetaloo Basin as a top priority. Environment groups have fiercely criticised the plan, saying it will "make climate change worse".

item.title: With almost 50 shops forced shut, can Perth's vape crackdown dent a huge black market?
item.description: Authorities say a recent two-week compliance blitz results in them seizing $11 million of illegal product like cigarettes and vapes, but how much is it disrupting the lucrative industry?

item.title: Maroons maul Blues in Melbourne to send Origin series to a decider
item.description: Queensland ignites with 36 points in the second half of State of Origin II to beat the Blues 44-24 and send the series to a decider.

item.title: Pakistani police pursue murder charge against officer who shot Perth girl Hania Ahmed
item.description: Pakistani officials apologise for the fatal shooting of a nine-year-old Australian girl, and outline the violation of protocols that led to her death as they pursue murder charges against the officer involved.

item.title: Aboriginal interpreters 'overlooked' by WA Police years after wrongful Josh Warneke murder conviction
item.description: More than a decade after Gene Gibson was wrongfully convicted for the murder of Broome tradesman Josh Warneke, Aboriginal interpreters say they are still overlooked by WA Police.

item.title: AFL fines Suns $20,000 for repeated umpire contact after Daniel Rioli incident
item.description: The AFL hands down a fine to the Suns after the club's fifth incident this season of a player making careless contact with an umpire.

item.title: Alleged RSL vandals granted a suppression on their images by Melbourne court
item.description: A court has suppressed the images of two women accused of graffitiing two RSLs in Melbourne for 12 months after their case is finalised.

item.title: Trial starts for Daryl Maguire over alleged involvement in fraudulent visa scheme
item.description: Daryl Maguire has pleaded not guilty to a charge of conspire to commit an offence over his alleged involvement in a fraudulent visa scheme.

item.title: Sarah Game's bill restricting late-term abortion access voted down in lower house
item.description: South Australia's lower house has voted against a bill to restrict access to abortions past 25 weeks. The legislation was defeated 36 votes to 9.

item.title: Young officers honoured for bravery after confronting armed man in fatal Nowra shooting
item.description: Four NSW police officers are formally recognised for their actions during a fatal shooting that a coroner found occurred while they acted to protect the community.

item.title: Ex-teacher's aide jailed after explicit Snapchat messages sent to teenagers
item.description: A former Tasmanian teacher's aide, who used social media to send sexually explicit content to teenagers, has been sentenced to 15 months' prison.

item.title: Alice Springs parole worker arrested sharing hotel bed with fugitive killer pleads guilty
item.description: A 37-year-old legal aid worker who was arrested while sharing a hotel bed with a teenage killer she helped evade authorities after he breached his parole has pleaded guilty to attempting to pervert the course of justice.

item.title: SpaceX rises, oil plunges and the ASX edges up
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: One Nation prepared to cut worker entitlements to safeguard struggling small businesses
item.description: One Nation's newly named "treasury spokesman" Barnaby Joyce says the party is prepared to cut worker entitlements to safeguard small businesses struggling to deal with inflation and high power prices.

item.title: Joel Davies stars in debut for Australia as they down Bangladesh in T20
item.description: Cooper Connolly and the Aussie spinners have guided Australia to a T20 win over Bangladesh with 10 balls to spare.

item.title: Japanese ice cream companies raided over alleged price fixing cartel
item.description: Six of Japan's biggest ice cream companies are under suspicion of forming a cartel to hike up prices.

item.title: MCG disaster pushes New South Wales to the brink of another State of Origin defeat
item.description: New South Wales had travelled to the MCG with the Origin series within their grasp, but after last night's disaster, success has never felt further away.

item.title: Perry leads Australia to T20 World Cup victory over Bangladesh
item.description: Ellyse Perry has starred with bat and ball as Australia gave their net run-rate a massive boost with a landslide win over Bangladesh at Headingley in the T20 World Cup.

item.title: State of Origin II quick hits: Selwyn Cobbo's hat-trick, Kotoni Staggs's sin-bin and Reece Walsh false starts
item.description: Billy Slater resists the urge to play his trump card as the Maroons recover from an inexcusable early blunder. Here are five quick hits from State of Origin II.

item.title: Energy watchdog wants power bills to become 'more like buying milk'
item.description: Millions of Australians have been often unwittingly moved to complex and variable power tariffs. An energy watchdog says bills must be simplified.

item.title: Qantas's Project Sunrise to launch first direct route between Sydney and London in 2027
item.description: The flights, set to begin take off from next year, will mark the first time flights from the east coast will complete the Kangaroo Route without a stop over.

item.title: Luigi Mangione to argue he suffered 'extreme emotional disturbance' in murder trial
item.description: The 28-year-old is accused of killing an American health insurance executive. If successful, his defence could see him convicted of manslaughter rather than murder.

item.title: Live: Tempers flare after Ghana scores in stoppage time to break Panama hearts
item.description: Ghana lost a goalkeeper but scored a last-gasp winner against Panama in Toronto, after Harry Kane nets a brace in England's entertaining 4-2 win over Croatia. Follow live.

item.title: US releases text of ceasefire deal struck with Iran
item.description: A senior US official says both sides of the war can still walk away from the agreement, which contains 14 points.

item.title: Leviticus turns loathing of queer love into the monster
item.description: Writer and director Adrian Chiarella's debut supernatural horror feature, Leviticus, is as romantic as it is scary.

item.title: Travel quiz, June 18: Grab your suitcase, we're going on a trip
item.description: Which airport hosts the world's largest indoor waterfall? What is the Swiss delicacy raclette?

item.title: Defiant Pauline Hanson gives Labor and the Coalition plenty to be nervous about
item.description: In one press club appearance, we've learned more about where Hanson stands. And that creates risk for One Nation.

item.title: Manuel is on a mission to end illegal gold mining in the Amazon
item.description: In Peru, I meet a man risking his life to end illegal gold mining in the Amazon, in the hope of saving his beloved town before it's too late.

item.title: Pauline Hanson's criticism of Guardian reporter labelled an 'assault on free press'
item.description: Pauline Hanson's public excoriation of a Guardian journalist questioning her at the National Press Club yesterday is an "assault on the freedom of the press", the media union says, as Labor and Liberal MPs criticise the One Nation leader for getting personal with journalists.

item.title: HSBC agrees to pay $35 million penalty after widespread scam failures
item.description: HSBC has admitted to failing to protect customers from scams and agreed to a $35 million penalty in the Federal Court.

item.title: KPMG the latest example of accountants being unaccountable
item.description: Bean counters have historically been viewed as dull, dull, dull. But since the turn of the century, accounting scandals have come thick and fast in Australia.

item.title: Franchisees ruined after 7-Eleven forces, then blocks sale of petrol station
item.description: Jotika and Sunny Sharma took out a loan to purchase their Sydney franchise in 2015. When the lease expired this year, their relationship with the convenience store giant turned ugly.

item.title: Don't expect a post-budget $1,250 boost this tax time
item.description: There was a lot of talk about the $1,000 instant tax reduction and the $250 Working Australians Tax Offset in the federal budget. But they won't be in play at tax time this year.

item.title: How students are getting through remote living while on mandatory placements
item.description: University student Tarhnee says her survival kit for a nursing prac placement was food, a swag, a chair and a little table for her laptop.

item.title: When are the Socceroos playing? Here's where the World Cup is taking them next
item.description: The Socceroos will face the USA in their second Group D match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Here's what time you'll need to set your alarm.

item.title: Energy industry sector weighs in on AI data centre risks to Marinus Link
item.description: Tasmania's cool climate and access to renewable energy has made it an attractive place for AI data centre investment, but energy industry voices are warning that the business case for the Marinus Link project appears increasingly unviable if power-hungry players such as AI data centres continue to expand across the state.

item.title: Tasmania Devils AFL coaching choice is a blessing and a curse
item.description: Buckley? Longmire? Hinkley? The Tasmania Devils need to pick a coach to get them ready for their AFL entry, and the list of potential picks presents a dilemma.

item.title: Aged care tool under fire as man with late-stage MND loses appeal for higher funding
item.description: Melbourne man Graham Crossan relies on his wife Gaynor to help him eat, move and even breathe. But a new assessment tool means he has been refused funding for more support.

item.title: Injured toddler's ordeal highlights problem of weather radar blackspot
item.description: When two-year-old Teddy suffered a suspected broken leg on his family's outback property, the journey to hospital was complicated by a lack of reliable weather forecasting.

item.title: Northern hairy-nosed wombats are 'picky' with their homes, but they may survive in other habitats
item.description: One of the world's most critically endangered animals may be able to live in more areas than conservationists realised.

item.title: Ellyse Perry dominates with the ball as Australia trounces Bangladesh at women's T20 World Cup
item.description: Ellyse Perry dominates with the ball as Australia waltzes home to victory over Bangladesh in the women's T20 World Cup.

item.title: Homeless woman feels safer living in vehicle than shared housing
item.description: Housing advocates say an increasing number of single, older women are choosing to live in vehicles while they wait for social housing.

item.title: David Littleproud calls for investigation into insurers in south-west Queensland
item.description: The MP has referred insurers, which he calls "corporate cancer", to the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission.

item.title: Convicted paedophile cleared to run family‑friendly Sydney apartment blocks
item.description: The NSW government has authorised a convicted paedophile to manage nearly a dozen apartment buildings across Sydney, including those popular with young families.

item.title: Live: ASX opens lower as US Federal Reserve leans towards rate hikes
item.description: The ASX has opened down this morning, with the US Federal Reserve deciding to keep interest rates on hold, but a growing group of committee members predict a rate hike this year.

item.title: Fugitive who fled to Lebanon after shooting named as one of NSW Police's most wanted
item.description: The alleged gunman slipped the net and fled to Lebanon days after he was paid hundreds of thousands of dollars to take out a Sydney underworld figure in 2022.

item.title: Gilgo Beach serial killer Rex Heuermann sentenced to life in prison without parole
item.description: A New York architect was sentenced to three consecutive life sentences plus 100 years after pleading guilty to the murders of at least eight women in Long Island's Gilgo Beach.

item.title: How Socceroo Nestory Irankunda got his start with Adelaide's Northern Wolves
item.description: Long before Nestory Irankunda's World Cup goal buoyed Australian fans he made his start with Adelaide's Northern Wolves Soccer Club, where he is a hero to many other young players who share a similar background.

item.title: Mount Gambier pair sentenced after neglecting more than 100 animals, including birds, cats and a dog
item.description: A South Australian couple is barred from owning pets after mistreating more than 100 animals, including cats, a dog, chickens and other birds.

item.title: Fremantle Dockers embrace premiership favouritism as club flexes muscle
item.description: Hard-bitten Dockers supporters won't want to say it out loud, but as Fremantle flexes more muscle with each passing week, hope is slowly turning to expectation that a first AFL flag could be on the cards.

item.title: Three months after major flooding in Katherine, this couple is still living on their back verandah
item.description: Katherine couple Nicki and Luke Asling have been living on their back verandah for the past three months, after their home was flooded to the ceiling in March. Local renovators say it will take months for all the repairs in town to be completed.

item.title: Live: CGT carve-outs will be available to 2.7 million small businesses, treasurer says
item.description: The prime minister has unveiled wideranging carve-outs for small businesses from the government's budget changes. Follow live.

item.title: US and Iran sign memorandum aimed at ending war
item.description: The presidents of the United States and Iran have officially signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at ending the war and reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Maroons Origin star Tino Fa'asuamaleaui reveals his dad's message to him before death
item.description: Tino Fa'asuamaleaui shares the advice his dad gave him before his death after a long battle with cancer.

item.title: Whyalla's giant Australian cuttlefish missing amid fears algal bloom to blame
item.description: Whyalla is the breeding spot for hundreds of thousands of cuttlefish, but the international spectacle has been cancelled after only dozens have been spotted this year.

item.title: State of Origin: Sam Walker's player of the match award is a recognition of what he represents
item.description: On Wednesday night, Queensland coach Billy Slater got a glimpse of a glistening Sam Walker-led future, even if the present wasn't quite perfect.

item.title: Dark Mofo platform exposing Tasmanian artists to wider audiences
item.description: Her headline show at Dark Mofo sold out in just over an hour, something Tasmanian Indigenous singer Miss Kaninna says couldn't have happened without the winter festival's ability to attract "massive, massive" music industry names.

item.title: Virginia Trioli to leave the ABC after 27 years
item.description: After 27 years as a journalist, broadcaster and program maker at the ABC, Virginia Trioli announces she is leaving to pursue new creative opportunities.

item.title: Live: 'It's signed': US and Iran agree to end war within 60 days
item.description: The US and Iran have signed a memorandum of understanding to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.

item.title: How one tree in WA's Kimberley went from a place of pain to one of healing
item.description: In Western Australia's far north there is a "whipping tree" which for decades stood as a reminder of painful times, but has now become a place of healing and reconciliation.

item.title: Beloved central Queensland theatre-restaurant sells up after 32 years in the biz
item.description: One of Queensland's last "dinner and a show" venues is selling up, following a long-running decline of theatre-restaurants across the country.

item.title: First Australian hantavirus cruise ship passenger speaks from quarantine
item.description: One of the Australians who was onboard the MV Hondius recounts the "huge shock" of a deadly outbreak on the ship, as he looks forward to leaving a Perth quarantine facility and getting a "big hug" from his family.

item.title: Australian universities dominate global rankings as report reveals staff discontent
item.description: Annual university rankings reveal the University of New South Wales is now the highest-rated Australian institution, placing 19th worldwide.

item.title: Mexican military shoots down drone at South Korea's World Cup training facility
item.description: Sources confirm Mexican authorities shot down a drone at South Korea's training facility as reports of suspect aircraft rise.

item.title: SpaceX rises, oil plunges & the ASX edges up
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: After years of reform, a preventable disease eradicated in 'white Australia' is still on the rise
item.description: Years after losing his sister to a preventable disease, a grieving Gulf of Carpentaria man is demanding urgent action to address a disease found almost exclusively in remote, Indigenous communities.

item.title: Bird flu kills thousands of baby seals on Heard Island in Southern Ocean
item.description: Research has revealed the devastating impact of bird flu on a remote Australian territory where more than 13,000 baby seals are now believed to have been killed by the virus.

item.title: Great Southern farmer combines love of farming and filmmaking to put her region in the spotlight
item.description: Farmer Brianna Jones has worked on some of Western Australia's biggest film productions, such as Blueback, Sweet As, and The Surfer.

item.title: Wout van Aert to miss Tour de France in major blow to Jonas Vingegaard's hopes
item.description: Wout van Aert confirms he will miss this year's Tour de France after injuring his elbow.

item.title: Vodafone's mobile network suffers hours-long outage, potentially affecting millions of Australians
item.description: A statement from the company said it was aware some customers were still experiencing "intermittent issues" with network coverage, but the cause of the problem had been "isolated and resolved".

item.title: Hot mics at G7 capture world leaders' chats between weighty topics
item.description: As world leaders made their way into conference rooms at a lakeside resort in Évian-les-Bains, microphones caught plenty of off-the-cuff banter.

item.title: Heartbroken parents of two children killed in Sydney car crash forgive driver, speak of loss
item.description: The heartbroken parents of two children killed when they were hit by a car in Sydney's south-west yesterday have spoken of their shock, saying they forgive the driver.

item.title: Live: ABC meteorologist Nate Byrne answers your questions about El Niño
item.description: With the Bureau of Meteorology formally declaring an El Niño event, two experts unpack what this will mean for Australia's weather in the coming months. Follow live.

item.title: SA government's $28m fund for farming assistance not used for a decade
item.description: A South Australian government fund of more than $28 million for loans and grants for farmers has not been used for the past 10 years, despite the industry struggling through drought and poor market conditions.

item.title: Murder charge dropped against Gold Coast woman Maree Mavis Crabtree over daughter's 2012 death
item.description: Several charges, including the murder of Erin Crabtree, were discontinued in the Supreme Court in Brisbane on Thursday.

item.title: Olympic bronze medallist Jenny Simpson undergoes CPR after collapsing during event
item.description: American Olympic medallist Jenny Simpson undergoes CPR after collapsing during a community running event.

item.title: Victoria's crime wave shows signs of falling, according to latest crime statistics
item.description: Figures for the year to the end of March show youth crime fell by 6 per cent in Victoria, but adult crime rose by around 10 per cent.

item.title: New lease with Morris Group announced for Double Island off Cairns coast
item.description: A new lease has been announced for the abandoned Double Island resort, and Brad Pitt, Keanu Reaves and Jennifer Aniston have been invited back for the reopening party.

item.title: Albanese and Chalmers unveil capital gains carve-outs for small businesses, startups
item.description: The federal government has consulted with small businesses and startups over contentious elements of its budget tax proposal.

item.title: Three pet dogs dead after suspected baiting in Echuca
item.description: The dogs died within hours of eating an unknown substance while being walked at a trotting track in a regional Victorian town.

item.title: Snowtown serial killer James Vlassakis's release on parole subject to further review
item.description: Another review will be held to decide whether Snowtown serial killer James Vlassakis should be released on parole.

item.title: Police arrest a man in Tocumwal over alleged child grooming offences
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tocumwal man arrested over alleged child grooming after NSW police operation
item.description: A 22-year-old man living on the NSW–Victorian border was arrested on Wednesday after detectives assumed the online identity of a girl.

item.title: World Cup chaos could be concern for England after Croatia win
item.description: England's World Cup opener against Croatia, with a missed penalty and a flaky defence, seemed to be briefly polluted by fear.

item.title: Jackie O and ARN Media legal battle continues after Kyle Sandilands's $12m settlement
item.description: A legal battle between Jackie "O" Henderson and ARN Media appears still headed for an October trial after her former co-host Kyle Sandilands settled his own court case against the company.

item.title: Drink driver who killed nine-year-old boy in Gold Coast crash sentenced to 14 years in jail
item.description: The court heard that on the day of the crash on the Gold Coast in 2024, 49-year-old Keith Andrew Chambers had mixed alcohol with painkillers he used to treat arthritis, as well as antidepressants.

item.title: Coroner finds 'no breakthrough' after inquest into death of Mark Haines
item.description: The death of an Indigenous teenager Mark Haines, whose body was discovered on railway tracks in northern NSW in 1988, remains a mystery despite a coronial inquest that stretched over 24 days.
